perturbation.original_text: (a) “Affiliates ” mean s entities controlling, controlled by or under common control with Vendor or with Company.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) “Affiliates ” mean s entities that have a business relationship with Vendor or with Company.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the definition of 'Affiliates.' Originally, it referred to entities with a control relationship. The changed definition broadens it to any entity with a business relationship. This contradicts later sections that rely on the original definition based on control when determining the scope of confidentiality obligations, especially section 2(c) where 'Affiliates' who need to know confidential information are subject to the same restrictions, implying a level of control rather than just any business relationship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Receiving Party may disclose the Con fidential Information to the employ- ees, officers, directors, and Affiliates of Receiving Party who need to know such Confi- dential Information in connection with the Purpose and who receive such information subject to the same or comparable restrictions as are contained in this Agreement as evi- denced by a signed non -disclosure agreement or equivalent . Disclosure of the Confiden- tial Information to outside agents and affiliates other than Receiving Party must be agreed upon by the Disclosing Party in writing .
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'Affiliates' from entities with a control relationship to any entity with a business relationship. This contradicts section 2(c), which implies a level of control over Affiliates when discussing the sharing of confidential information. The original definition based on control ensured that confidential information was only shared with entities under the Receiving Party's control, subject to the same restrictions. The new definition could include entities with a mere business relationship, who may not be subject to the same level of control and confidentiality obligations, making the agreement unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall not alter or remove any confidentiality or proprietary marking on the Confidential Information and , subject to the forego- ing, may make such limited number of copies of Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to the ex tent necessary to achieve the Purpose. Within 15 days of written request from Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall promptly return Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and all copies thereof to Disclosing Party or destroy Disclosing Party’s Conf idential Information, in- cluding all copies thereof, purge its computer systems of such Confidential Information and cer- tify such destruction in writing signed by a duly authorized representative .
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall not alter or remove any confidentiality or proprietary marking on the Confidential Information and , subject to the forego- ing, may make such limited number of copies of Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to the ex tent necessary to achieve the Purpose. Within 90 days of written request from Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall promptly return Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and all copies thereof to Disclosing Party or destroy Disclosing Party’s Conf idential Information, in- cluding all copies thereof, purge its computer systems of such Confidential Information and cer- tify such destruction in writing signed by a duly authorized representative .
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation changes the timeframe for returning confidential information from 15 days to 90 days. This creates an in-text contradiction with the general expectation of prompt action concerning confidential information, potentially conflicting with the 'reasonable care' standard mentioned in Section 2(d), especially if the confidential information is time-sensitive.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) Each party shall use at least the same degree of care, but in no event less than reasonable care, to avoid inadvertent disclosure or unpermitted use of the other par- ty's Confidential Information which it employs with respect to its own proprietar y or con- fidential information of a similar nature which it does not wish to have disseminated, published or disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the timeframe for returning confidential information from 15 days to 90 days. The explanation indicates that this contradicts Section 2(d), which requires 'reasonable care' to avoid disclosure, because a delay of 90 days might not be considered reasonable, especially if the information is time-sensitive. The original text's 15-day timeframe aligns more closely with the 'reasonable care' standard than the new 90-day timeframe, thus creating a direct conflict with the 'reasonable care' requirement in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Dat e and shall expire three years’ thereafter, unless earlier terminated by either party upon at least ten days’ prior notice to the other par ty. The restrictions and obligations set forth in this Agreement shall survive any expiration or termination for five years from the date of such expiration or termination with respect to Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement prior to th e date of such expiration or termination .
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Dat e and shall expire three years’ thereafter, unless earlier terminated by either party upon at least thirty days’ prior notice to the other par ty. The restrictions and obligations set forth in this Agreement shall survive any expiration or termination for one year from the date of such expiration or termination with respect to Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement prior to th e date of such expiration or termination .
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation alters the notice period for early termination from ten days to thirty days and reduces the survival period for confidentiality obligations from five years to one year after termination. This contradicts the intention of a long-term protection of Confidential Information which is explicitly stated throughout the NDA. It also creates an in-text contradiction by diminishing the protection offered to the Disclosing Party, contrary to the purpose outlined in the initial 'WHEREAS' clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, each party desires to provide and to receive information that may be consid- ered confidential or proprietary; and WHEREAS, prior to providing such information, each party requires a Non -Disclosure Agreement from the other party; NOW, THEREFORE, to ensure protection of such information and for other considera- tion the receipt and sufficiency of which is hereby acknowledged, the parties do hereby agree as follows: 1. Definitions .
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated a ten-day notice for termination and a five-year survival period for confidentiality obligations. The changed text increases the notice period to thirty days and reduces the survival period to one year. The 'WHEREAS' clauses in the contradicted text state the agreement is 'to ensure protection of such information'. The shortened survival period in the changed text directly undermines this stated purpose, creating a clear contradiction because it offers less protection than initially intended. The reduction of protection contradicts the aim to 'ensure protection of such information', and represents an inconsistency, as per the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon the parties, their successors and as- signs. Neither party shall assign this Agreement or any Confidential Information re- ceived from the ot her party pursuant to this Agreement without the other party's prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon the parties, their successors and as- signs. Either party may assign this Agreement or any Confidential Information re- ceived from the ot her party pursuant to this Agreement with the other party's prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by changing "Neither party" to "Either party" on the assignment clause. Before this clause, the contract states that 'Neither party shall assign this Agreement or any Confidential Information received from the other party pursuant to this Agreement without the other party's prior written consent.' The new statement means that 'Either party may assign this Agreement or any Confidential Information received from the other party pursuant to this Agreement with the other party's prior written consent.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) This Agreement shall be binding upon the parties, their successors and as- signs. Either party may assign this Agreement or any Confidential Information re- ceived from the ot her party pursuant to this Agreement with the other party's prior written consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Neither party shall assign...' while the changed text states 'Either party may assign...'. This introduces a direct contradiction because the original text prohibits assignment without consent from both parties, while the changed text permits either party to assign with the other's consent. The contradicted text is the exact same statement as the changed text, meaning that it contradicts the original intention of the document where neither party could assign the agreement, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Whenever under the terms of or in connection with this Agreement any notice, consent, approval, authorization or other information is proper or required to be given by either party, such notice, consent, approval, authorization or other information shall be in writing and shall be given or made by facsimile, by reputable overnight courier with documentation of receipt to the intended recipient th ereof or by registered or certified mail, return receipt re- quested, and with all postage prepaid, addressed as follows:
perturbation.changed_text: Whenever under the terms of or in connection with this Agreement any notice, consent, approval, authorization or other information is proper or required to be given by either party, such notice, consent, approval, authorization or other information shall be in writing and shall be given or made by email, by reputable overnight courier with documentation of receipt to the intended recipient th ereof or by registered or certified mail, return receipt re- quested, and with all postage prepaid, addressed as follows:
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation replaces facsimile with email as an acceptable form of written notice. While seemingly minor, this introduces ambiguity with Section 6, which specifies that if notice is given by facsimile, a hard copy must also be sent within two days. This requirement is not addressed for email, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the necessary steps for proper notification and potential disputes over whether an email notice is sufficient on its own. As well as creates contradictions due to this change, given section 6 also mentions the need for documentation of receipt
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notice shall be deemed received o n the date of receipt or Page 4 of 5 LiION Mutual NDA.docx Form updated: 2.27.2015 the date on which receipt is refused. In the event notice is given by facsimile, a copy of such fac- simile shall be sent to the recipient thereof in accordance with the provisions hereof (other than by facsimile) within two days af ter such facsimile was transmitted.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces facsimile with email as an acceptable form of written notice. The contradicted text specifies that if notice is given by facsimile, a hard copy must also be sent within two days. Since the changed text adds email as an acceptable form of written notice but does not mention a hard copy requirement as the contradicted text does, it creates an inconsistency and contradiction regarding the necessary steps for proper notification. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as it provides conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges that it has had the opportunity to have legal counsel review this Agreement and to negotiate its terms and conditions. Should any questions of construction or interpretation of this Agreement arise, the parties agree that no presumption shall be applied against the party drafting this Agreement or any portion thereof and that the language of this Agreement shall, in all cases, be construed as a whole according t o its fair meaning and not strictly for or against either party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges that it has had the opportunity to have legal counsel review this Agreement and to negotiate its terms and conditions. Should any questions of construction or interpretation of this Agreement arise, the parties agree that presumption shall be applied against the party drafting this Agreement or any portion thereof and that the language of this Agreement shall, in all cases, be construed as a whole according t o its fair meaning and not strictly for or against either party.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation changes 'no presumption shall be applied' to 'presumption shall be applied'. It creates an in-text contradiction by negating a neutral interpretation clause which favors fairness for both parties, and by implying that if an ambiguity exist, such ambiguity will be held agains the drafter (the opposite of the original agreement) therefore this change would be against the principle of fairness.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (j) Each party acknowledges that it has had the opportunity to have legal counsel review this Agreement and to negotiate its terms and conditions. Should any questions of construction or interpretation of this Agreement arise, the parties agree that presumption shall be applied against the party drafting this Agreement or any portion thereof and that the language of this Agreement shall, in all cases, be construed as a whole according t o its fair meaning and not strictly for or against either party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'no presumption shall be applied' to 'presumption shall be applied', which directly contradicts the original intent of fair interpretation for both parties. The contradicted text, which is in fact the same changed text in this example, highlights this contradiction within the document. The changed text now states that presumption *will* be applied against the drafter, conflicting with the principle of fairness.
